title_original: PNNL, NVIDIA to Add GPU Acceleration to Quantum Computing
summary_original: The Pacific Northwest National Laboratory has partnered with NVIDIA to create an open-source framework that connects graphics processing unit acceleration to quantum-classical computing using the NVIDIA NVQLink platform. Announced at NVIDIA GTC 2026, this initiative integrates NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips with a field-programmable gate array-based measurement and control system for quantum experimentation. The framework aims to lower barriers for researchers by enabling high-throughput computing and minimal delay—crucial for precise timing and fast data processing in quantum experiments. According to project lead Sam Stein, the open system architecture leverages high-performance classical graphics processing units to meet the intensive real-time computing needs of quantum processors, providing practical support for testing and refining near-term quantum systems.
